content.full_text:
September 27, 1989


Andrew Getz
1355 Ocean Avenue
Emeryville, CA 94608

Re: Letter No. 89-551

Dear Mr. Getz:

We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on September 21, 1989. Your letter
has been assigned to Jill Stecher for response. If you have any
questions, you may contact her directly at (916) 322-5901.

If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh:confadvl


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Andrew Getz
1355 Ocean Avenue
Emeryville, California 94608
(415) 652-4191

FPPC

SEP 21 1 40 PM '89

September 20, 1989

7-829-2511

Ms. Jill Stecher
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Stecher,

Thank you for taking the time to discuss and research the question
of whether or not an official's residence is exempt from the
conflict of interest restrictions which apply to other real
property equity.

As I understand your interpretation, an official can have a
"financial interest" in his or her primary residence under
Political Reform Act §87103.

The exception contained in §87206(f) applies only to Form 721
disclosure, and does not exempt one's residence from being
"financial interest" capable of creating a conflict of interest.

Please let me know if the above does not accurately reflect your
interpretation of the Act. Again, thank you for researching this
question for me.

Very truly yours,

Andrew Getz
Andrew Getz
